name: Guns N' Roses
bio: Guns N' Roses remains one of the strongest hard-rock catalogs in streaming, with a handful of global evergreen tracks doing most of the economic work.
notable_for: Guns N' Roses remains closely associated with Sweet Child O' Mine and November Rain, which continue to anchor catalog attention.
career_highlight: Songs like Sweet Child O' Mine and November Rain still help define the catalog's long-tail earnings profile.
revenue_drivers: Classic catalog streaming keeps major songs active well beyond the original release cycle.
Playlist longevity and generational rediscovery support steady long-tail listening.
Film, television, sports, and trailer usage can reactivate demand for familiar recordings.

earnings.assumptions: Estimate keeps Guns N' Roses's current headline range as the artist-side figure and models gross catalog, label, publishing, and writer lanes from that conservative annual range.

ownership.master_owner: Likely split across label, distributor, and artist-affiliated rights depending on recording era
ownership.publishing_owner: Writer and publisher splits materially affect final artist-side income
ownership.catalog_sale_status: No full catalog sale assumption baked into this modeled range
ownership.notes: Guns N' Roses's page should be read as modeled artist-side annual income, not a public royalty statement. Ownership and label terms can materially change take-home economics.
